_meta.name: arabic-fix
_meta.description: Pure-JSON design tokens for Arabic UI. Logical-properties only, RTL-aware, real Arabic fonts, BANNED letter-spacing on Arabic runs.
_meta.format: W3C Design Tokens Community Group (draft, $value/$type/$description) + Style Dictionary 3+ compatible

display.$description: Display headings in Arabic. Tajawal has 7 weights for tonal variation; IBM Plex Sans Arabic is the workhorse for headers.

body.$description: Body text in Arabic. IBM Plex Sans Arabic ships with regular, semibold, bold — sufficient for 90% of products. Tajawal is the geo-alt.

mono.$description: Monospace Arabic for code blocks, technical content. Vazirmatn Mono covers U+0600-U+06FF + presentation forms.

naskh.$description: Naskh serif for Quran samples, long-form editorial, religious / classical text. Amiri ships tashkeel intentionally.

mixed.$description: Bilingual UI strings where Latin and Arabic share a line. Inter has Latin only; Arabic falls through to IBM Plex Sans Arabic.

regular.$description: Default Arabic body weight. Note: 400 in Arabic has more visual mass than 400 in Latin.

semibold.$description: Use for emphasis. Visual equivalent of Latin 700 — chosen because 700 in Arabic reads heavy next to 400 Latin.

bold.$description: Display headings only. 800/900 in Arabic look synthetic — fonts rarely ship those glyphs and the renderer must interpolate.

base.$description: Default body size. 16px matches user-agent minimum.

arabic.$description: Arabic body line-height. Higher than the Latin 1.5 default because tashkeel (diacritics) needs vertical breathing room. Citations: UAX #29 (text segmentation, last revised 2024).

enable.$description: Kashida (tatweel, U+0640) justification policy. 'auto' = browser-typographer-controlled, preserves get-equal-equal-justified Arabic paragraphs without distorting letter shapes.

xs.$description: Tight inline-start padding (chips, badges). Resolves to right padding under dir=rtl.
Tight inline-end padding. Resolves to left padding under dir=rtl.

md.$description: Default block-start padding (vertical, top in LTR). Resolves to top regardless of dir.
Default block-end padding (vertical, bottom in LTR).

row.$description: Default vertical gap between Arabic list items / paragraphs.

column.$description: Default horizontal gap between Arabic fields / cards.

lg.$description: Major layout gutter. Use between primary sections in a multi-column RTL layout.

padding-inline.$description: Container horizontal padding. Symmetric in any direction.

default.$description: Default document direction for Arabic locales. Set on <html dir="rtl"> or the root layout container.
Logical alignment keyword. Resolves to right in dir=rtl, left in dir=ltr. NEVER hardcode 'left' or 'right' for Arabic paragraphs.

arabic.$description: EXPLICITLY BANNED on Arabic runs. letter-spacing > 0 adds space on the wrong side in RTL and breaks the Arabic baseline. Use word-spacing.arabic instead. Downstream linters must flag any non-zero value here as an error.
Default inter-word spacing for Arabic paragraphs. 0.05em is enough to separate words joined by ligature chain without breaking the visual flow.
Default first-line indent for Arabic long-form text. 2ch matches the Arabic typographic convention (قرآن, news editorials). Western 'first-line: 1.25em' is too aggressive in Arabic.
Allows Arabic comma (،) and Arabic semicolon (؛) to hang in the margin in justified text, matching Naskh tradition. UAX #9 (BiDi, last revised 2025) handles the punctuation runs.
Use kashida (tatweel) as primary justification mechanism. 'inter-word' = fall back to word-spacing when kashida is unavailable. 'inter-character' is forbidden on Arabic.
Disable automatic hyphenation on Arabic (no Unicode soft-hyphen rules support Arabic). 'manual' allows soft-hyphen (U+00AD) but not browser-driven breaks.

normalize.$description: Preserve user-authored newlines, collapse other whitespace. Use to keep Markdown-style line breaks in user content.

arabic.$description: Break long Arabic URLs / IDs at word boundary. Without this, long mixed-script strings overflow RTL containers.
OpenType features for Arabic. liga+calt+rlig produce lam-alef (لا) and Allah (الله) contextual ligatures. kern improves letter-pair spacing. Required for Amiri / IBM Plex Sans Arabic to look right.

text-orientation.$description: Mixed orientation: horizontal for LTR runs, horizontal for Arabic runs. 'upright' is reserved for vertical-only scripts and would break Arabic readability.

bidi-isolation.$description: CSS direction-isolation behavior for embedded LTR / RTL runs. 'isolate' prevents surrounding BiDi from leaking into the isolated run. Required for @user mentions, file paths, version tags embedded in Arabic text. Citations: UAX #9 §BD13.

tab-size.$description: Tab stop width. Arabic code blocks (Python, JavaScript) use this. 4 matches the Python and JS community default.

light.$description: Default body text on light surface. WCAG AA contrast on #f7f8fa.

dark.$description: Default body text on dark surface. WCAG AA contrast on #1f2933.

light.$description: Text on accent.primary. White at AA contrast on #1e3a8a.

dark.$description: Text on accent.primary-dark. Near-black at AA contrast on the lighter dark-mode accent.

light.$description: App background. Slightly cool to avoid harsh pure white.

dark.$description: App background in dark mode. Charcoal, not pure black, to preserve shadow detail.

light.$description: Deep navy — chosen over bright #2563eb because high-saturation blue on white fails WCAG AA against dense Arabic text.

dark.$description: Soft blue-100 — light enough to read on dark, no need to flip text color.

light.$description: Deep crimson. Avoid neon red (#ef4444) which triggers Arabic color-term confusion (أحمر قرمزي vs أحمر صارخ).

fast.$description: Hover, focus-visible. Anything faster than 100ms feels broken on Arabic text.

decelerate.$description: Entrance easing — incoming elements accelerate from rest.

accelerate.$description: Exit easing — outgoing elements accelerate from rest to full speed.

common.$description: GPU-accelerated properties only. Animating color / background-color on Arabic text causes kerning flicker.

inline-rtl.$description: Translate-X token resolved via logical transform: in RTL, 'transform: translateX(2px)' moves elements LEFT (toward inline-start). Use CSS logical property: 'translate: var(--motion-transform-translate-inline-rtl) 0' or wrap with 'transform: scaleX(-1)' when mirroring.

direction.$description: Default animation direction. 'reverse' in RTL does not mirror visual motion — use 'animation-direction: alternate' for ping-pong effects that respect BiDi.
